permissions.comment: Read any file the user picks for the context bin. A reference file can live anywhere the user browses (Documents, but also project dirs like C:\dev), so read is broad by necessity; narrow it to e.g. $HOME/** if you only ever add files from home. Read only, no write.

Voice temp file, and temp copies of HTML-picked images to convert (the native file dialog returns null on some machines, so images come in as browser File objects with bytes but no path; we stage them here for ffmpeg).
Create the temp staging dir for HTML-picked image conversions.

allow.comment: Const-me whisper transcription. Resolved from the bundled resource dir ($RESOURCE); Whisper.dll sits next to main.exe in the same folder.
allow.name: whisper-run

allow.comment: Full ffmpeg build (gyan.dev 'full' static exe) for local image format conversion. args:true because the flags are built per output format (codec/quality/scale) in imageConvert.ts; the cmd is fixed to our bundled binary.
allow.name: ffmpeg-run

allow.comment: Dev fallback for ffmpeg. Under `tauri dev` the bundled $RESOURCE copy is often absent (the resource copy does not re-run when the exe is dropped into the glob dir), so this points at the in-tree source relative to the working dir (src-tauri under `tauri dev`). Harmless in production: that relative path does not exist there, so `ffmpeg-run` is chosen instead. imageConvert.ts probes prod first and only uses this if it works.
allow.name: ffmpeg-run-dev

allow.comment: Second dev fallback, same purpose as ffmpeg-run-dev but resolved from the repo root, in case `tauri dev` runs the binary with cwd at the project root rather than src-tauri.
allow.name: ffmpeg-run-dev-root

allow.comment: ImageMagick (magick.exe) as the HEIC/HEIF decode fallback: our ffmpeg build reports heif=false, so HEIC inputs route here instead. args:true because flags are built per output format (quality/resize) in imageConvert.ts; the cmd is fixed to our bundled binary. Wired exactly like ffmpeg-run.
allow.name: magick-run

allow.comment: Dev fallback for magick, same rationale as ffmpeg-run-dev: under `tauri dev` the bundled $RESOURCE copy is often absent, so this points at the in-tree source relative to the working dir (src-tauri under `tauri dev`). Harmless in production. imageConvert.ts probes prod first and only uses this if it works.
allow.name: magick-run-dev

allow.comment: Second dev fallback for magick, resolved from the repo root, in case `tauri dev` runs with cwd at the project root rather than src-tauri.
allow.name: magick-run-dev-root

permissions.comment: Image conversion picks a non-colliding output name next to the input; existence check only, no read/write of contents.
